name: maquette
description: Image-guided brand kits, component libraries, and implemented website pages

interface.shortDescription: Generate brand kits, components, and pages with image-first QA
interface.longDescription: Create website brand kits, reusable component libraries, and implemented pages through an image_gen-first contract-driven workflow.
defaultPrompt: Use $maquette to make a homepage for a fictional sparkling beverage brand called Fizz & Co. Run the full workflow: brand kit, component library, then homepage. Include product cards, marketing sections, newsletter signup, footer social links, and original generated image assets where useful.

Use $maquette-brand-kit to create a focused foundational website brand kit for a boutique snack brand. Generate and inspect a brand board first, then produce design-system JSON, CSS tokens, and approval notes.

Use $maquette-components to build a reusable component library from the existing approved brand kit. Process focused 1:1 component artifacts sequentially: check optional QA tooling, generate one text-only CSS-contract poster by default, write concrete category-prefixed componentized batch replica/reference, CSS/JS, catalog, and screenshot-reviewed artifacts, then move to the next artifact. Generate visual component sheets only when explicitly requested or when the CSS-contract route is documented as blocked. Include buttons, forms, cards, product cards, newsletter modules, footer/social modules, responsive navigation, and reference QA.

Use $maquette-pages to create a pricing page from the existing approved brand kit and component library. Reuse existing components first, request focused missing component coverage only if needed, then implement and QA the page.

Use $maquette-pages to create a product listing page from the existing Maquette artifacts. Check product-card coverage, align repeated card actions, use recognizable footer social icons, and record responsive QA.
